name: Bank Of Montana
description_short: Montana-based FDIC-insured bank specializing in business banking, multifamily financing, aviation loans, and government-enhanced lending programs for entrepreneurs and commercial clients.
description_long:
Bank of Montana is a regional bank headquartered in Missoula, Montana, that positions itself as 'The National Leader in Business Banking.' The bank was recognized by MSN Money as one of 359 Safest Banks in America and is one of only three such banks in Montana. It operates as an FDIC-insured institution backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of deposit and loan services with a primary focus on business and commercial lending. Core offerings include business banking accounts, personal loans, private banking services for eligible clients, multifamily financing, aviation financing, small business loans, and export-import financing through EXIM guarantees. They also provide access to government-enhanced loan programs in conjunction with the Montana Board of Investments, Small Business Administration, U.S. Department of Agriculture, and other state and federal programs.

Bank of Montana distinguishes itself through specialized lending expertise in several niches. They are one of only three HUD Multifamily-approved lenders in a six-state region and the only actual U.S. Export-Import Bank Guaranteed Lender in Montana. Their aviation financing division operates as one of only a few specialized aviation lenders nationally, staffed by experienced pilots. The bank emphasizes a relationship-based approach with dedicated relationship managers and premium interest rates for private banking clients.

While the bank clearly serves a specific market segment focused on business and commercial clients, consumers should note that this is not a retail banking institution catering to general banking needs. Their specialization in business aviation, multifamily housing finance, and export-import financing suggests their products are designed for entrepreneurs and commercial borrowers rather than traditional personal banking customers. The website provides limited detail on standard consumer products like checking accounts, savings rates, or personal loan terms.
services: Small business startup and growth loans
pros: FDIC-insured and recognized as one of 359 Safest Banks in America by MSN Money
Only HUD Multifamily-approved lender in a six-state region, providing specialized multifamily housing finance expertise
Only actual U.S. Export-Import Bank Guaranteed Lender in Montana for international business expansion
Specialized aviation financing division staffed by experienced pilots who understand aviation lending
24-hour access to personal relationship managers for private banking clients with premium interest rates
Access to government-enhanced loan programs (SBA, USDA, Montana Board of Investments, and other federal/state programs)
Customized loan solutions designed for small business and entrepreneur-specific needs
cons: Primarily a business-focused bank with limited information about consumer-oriented banking products
Appears to require relationship qualification or minimum standards for private banking services with premium rates
No clear information about standard deposit products, account minimums, or personal checking/savings rates on the public website
Limited geographic reach as a regional Montana bank despite national lending focus
No indication of online banking features, mobile apps, or digital banking convenience details
best_for: Business owners and entrepreneurs seeking specialized SBA and government-enhanced loan programs
Aircraft owners and aviation businesses requiring expert specialized financing
Multifamily property developers and investors seeking HUD-approved financing
International business owners looking for export-import financing and EXIM guarantees
diagnosis: Bank of Montana is best suited for business owners, entrepreneurs, and commercial borrowers—particularly those in aviation, multifamily real estate, or international trade—who benefit from specialized lending expertise and government-enhanced loan programs. General consumers seeking standard retail banking should note this bank's primary focus is commercial and business lending, not consumer-oriented banking services.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
